sections.paragraph:
m. a turtle dove (an onomatopoetic and primitive word), Gen 15:9 Levit. 12:6 used as a word of endearment for a beloved female, Son 2:12 [?]; used of the people of Israel; Psa 74:19, תֹּורְךָ “thy turtle dove,” i.e. the people especially dear to thee, now afflicted and timid.

II. תֹּור m.-

(1) order, row, turn, especially used of what goes round in a circle, Est 2:12, 15 .

(2) a string of pearls, or gold or silver beads (as an ornament for the head), Son 1:10, from the root תּוּר.

III, תֹּור 1Ch 17:17 i.q. תֹּורָה in the parallel place, 2Sa 7:19, mode, manner. If the reading be genuine, the form would seem to be from תֹּורֶה=תֹּורָה.

תור

Chald. an ox, i.q. Hebr. שֹׁור; plur. תֹּרִין oxen, Dan 4:22, 29 , 30 5:21 Ezr 6:9, 17 Ezr 6:17 7:17 .
